verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The U.S.–Iran conflict is now in its ninth straight night of strikes with the American troop death toll at 17, and oil has surged — Brent near $90, WTI in the low-$80s (up ~30% from July lows). But an Iranian diplomacy signal over the weekend eased the panic and equity futures are actually pointing higher (S&P +0.5%, Nasdaq +1%) as chipmakers bounce. VIX is elevated near 18–19, and P(≥1% gap) sits at 34% — just under the 35% trigger, composite at 52 (WARM). A live open-ended war plus an oil shock lock GREEN out even with futures green: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and mega-cap prints (GOOGL/TSLA Wed 7/22).

focus.jbig: Iran conflict 9th night · oil near $84/$90 · diplomacy hopes · half size
focus.jtag: Ninth straight night of U.S. strikes on Iran and rising oil — but a diplomacy signal lifts futures and chips bounce
focus.jnote: Two forces are pulling the tape in opposite directions. On one side, the U.S. completed its ninth consecutive night of strikes on Iran, the American troop death toll has climbed to 17, and Tehran declared its ceasefire effectively collapsed and said it intercepted four vessels in the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend — oil has now surged roughly 30% from its July lows, with Brent near $90 and WTI in the low-$80s. On the other side, investor sentiment improved after an Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman said intermediaries continued to exchange messages and negotiations could be pursued, and chipmakers traded higher to give equity markets a boost — S&P futures up ~0.5%, Nasdaq-100 up ~1%. Gold slipped below $4,000 toward nine-month lows as the dollar firmed and rate-hike fears rose. This is a live, headline-driven tape — deploy carefully, not aggressively.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and refiners keep catching a supply-fear bid, and chips (SMH, and names bouncing off last week’s rout) are stabilizing this morning. But energy is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper — one credible diplomacy headline (Iran is already signaling) gaps crude back down. Watch-only, and any spread needs the 9:35 validation to hold. Half size into a still-live Iran backdrop.
focus.notup: Airlines & fuel-sensitive transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) are pressured by the jet-fuel spike — Ryanair already flagged unhedged fuel costs more than doubling. But selling bear calls into a VIX near 18–19 with a diplomacy squeeze risk overhead is exactly the snap-back the 4-VAL rule exists to prevent. The disciplined answer is to wait, not chase the short.
focus.why: Why YELLOW? Composite 52 is in WARM and P(≥1% gap) at 34% sits just under the 35% trigger — both would allow GREEN on their own, and futures are even green this morning on diplomacy hope. What locks GREEN out is the combination: a live, open-ended U.S.–Iran military conflict in its ninth night with oil up ~30% off July lows and rising rate-hike odds (~53% for September). Neither is a scheduled, size-around event. The play: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and mega-cap prints (GOOGL/TSLA Wed 7/22), and validate at 9:35 before deploying.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~18–19, elevated; closed 18.77 Fri (+12%), 52-wk range 13.38–35.30, still off the highs
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines; GOOGL/TSLA/INTC earnings Wed 7/22, ECB meeting, flash PMIs
World risk — ninth straight night of U.S.–Iran strikes, ceasefire collapsed, 17 U.S. deaths, Hormuz vessels intercepted
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; curve sensitive to any Iran/oil shock, but diplomacy signal easing the spike
Commodities — oil up ~30% off July lows, Brent ~$90 / WTI low-$80s on Hormuz risk; the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: near 2-mo high · oil lifts rate-hike bets

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news — crude is up ~30% off July lows and energy is the day’s clearest supply-fear bid. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: Iran is already floating diplomacy through intermediaries, and one credible de-escalation headline gaps crude back $5–7 in minutes, taking your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a YELLOW day, don’t chase a war premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Chips are bouncing this morning after last week’s brutal rout (SMH −9% over the period, SOX −4% Thursday). But a one-morning bounce is not a durable catalyst — it’s a reflex rally in a group that has whipsawed all week, and GOOGL/TSLA/INTC report Wed 7/22, dropping mega-cap AI-capex risk right into any DTE. Selling premium into a stabilization attempt ahead of the season’s biggest prints is not a probability edge. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: A bounce isn’t a catalyst, and mega-cap earnings land Wed. Don’t underwrite a chip rebound into GOOGL/TSLA week.

setups.read: Jet fuel jumped with crude — Ryanair already flagged its unhedged fuel cost more than doubled and shares fell ~5.6% — so the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a diplomacy/peace headline squeezes shorts violently (Iran is signaling now), and VIX near 18–19 makes any bear call a snap-back hazard. A bear call into a live-war, VIX-elevated tape is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Diplomacy-squeeze risk into a fuel headwind = wait. Don’t chase the short until the tape settles.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this Monday morning. The mega-cap wave (Alphabet, Tesla, Intel, IBM, TXN, NOW) reports Wed–Thu this week — nothing has reported-and-gapped in the last 24–48h that qualifies for a Type A. Check back Thursday once the big prints hit the tape.
setups.check_note: Nothing to validate — no Type A candidate today.

setups.read: A war-headline, oil-shock tape is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts. The oil refiners that hit all-time highs mid-July (Valero, Marathon, Phillips 66, Par Pacific) are now outside their 4-session windows and are war-premium moves, not calm-tape breakouts. No fresh high-volume ATH breakout is holding into today’s open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-driven chop, diplomacy vs. strikes
scenarios.sub: Tape lurches on every Iran/oil headline; chips try to stabilize, energy bid holds, vol stays elevated but off highs

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation / Hormuz disruption
scenarios.sub: Iran hits a base or Hormuz shipping is disrupted further; oil runs toward $95–100, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Good case — Diplomacy breakthrough
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel deal; oil gives back the spike, a relief rally in stocks and chips, vol eases hard

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran / Strait of Hormuz — the U.S. completed its ninth straight night of strikes, the troop death toll is at 17, and Iran said it intercepted four vessels in the strait over the weekend. Any wider strike or closure sends crude and jet fuel vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

Oil near $84/$90 & the 10-year — WTI up ~30% off July lows keeps inflation risk alive despite cooler CPI/PPI; the 10Y is near a two-month high ~4.54% and markets price ~53% odds of a September Fed hike (up from 47%).

Diplomacy whipsaw — Iran is signaling willingness to negotiate through intermediaries. A breakthrough would gap oil and war-trades violently the other way — brutal for anyone short the squeeze or long the premium.

Mega-cap earnings · GOOGL & TSLA (Wed 7/22) — the first Magnificent Seven prints, plus INTC, IBM, TXN, NOW; TSLA options price a ~7.6% swing. Any AI-capex surprise could reignite the chip selloff. Keep spreads clear of each name’s print.
ECB meeting & global flash PMIs this week — macro catalysts stacked on top of the war and earnings. A hawkish surprise or weak PMI would compound the risk-off pressure.

earnings.intro: Q2 season goes mega-cap this week — Alphabet, Tesla and Intel headline Wednesday 7/22 after the close, with IBM, ServiceNow, Texas Instruments and more around it. With the Iran conflict still live and oil surging, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Consensus ~$101B revenue, ~$2.10 EPS. Cloud growth and AI-capex guidance are the swing factors — Alphabet just did an ~$80B raise to fund AI, so any capex or margin surprise moves the whole tech tape. First read of the mega-cap season.
rows.angle: Do not hold a GOOGL spread through Wednesday’s print. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Thursday.

rows.why: Consensus ~$26.4B rev, ~$0.54 EPS on record 480K deliveries — but focus is on automotive gross margins amid aggressive promo pricing. Stock is down ~15% YTD and trading below key EMAs. High-beta gap risk into a jumpy tape.
rows.angle: Do not carry a TSLA spread through Wednesday. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next session.

rows.why: Intel is down ~13% on the week in the chip selloff and its AI-chip roadmap is under scrutiny. Any capex or AI-spend surprise across the cluster could reignite the memory rout — stacking single-stock risk on the live Iran backdrop.
rows.angle: Keep any tech spread to a DTE that clears the earnings date — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail plus the ECB/PMI overlap.

earnings.outro: Mon 7/20 – Tue 7/21: quieter names and off-cycle reports; the tape trades the Iran/oil headlines and chip bounce. It gets real Wednesday 7/22 with mega-cap tech (Alphabet, Tesla, Intel) after the close, alongside the ECB decision and global flash PMIs. Half size, A-grade only, until the tape settles.
